setup.title: Setup
setup.intro: Touchstone doesn't search — it asks something else to search. Pick the backend that exposes the POST /retrieve contract, then load your set of questions.
setup.kNote: A generous k favours recognition: you tick among candidates rather than recalling from memory.
setup.loadHint: From a <mono>.txt</mono> or <mono>.json</mono> file, or typed on the fly below.
setup.importError: Couldn't read that file — expected .txt (one per line) or .json.
setup.addPlaceholder: Add a question on the fly…
setup.resumable: Resumable session · every answer is written immediately (append-only).

annotate.flowHint: Tick the passages that answer the question.
annotate.retrieved: {{n}} candidates retrieved (k) · sorted by descending score
annotate.answerPlaceholder: Summarise the correct answer from the ticked passages…

overview.title: Overview
overview.note: We also store the full list of shown candidates (not just the ticked ones) — needed to interpret recall correctly. Click a row to reopen the question.
overview.chunksNote: Questions beyond k are handled by widening to the page — we don't raise k for everyone.
overview.footnote: These are descriptive stats of the golden set, to calibrate k and the threshold — not evaluation metrics (recall@k, faithfulness), which are computed with ranx / Ragas from the export.

export.overline: Final step · export the gold standard
export.title: Export
export.intro: The human builds the gold standard, the machine then reuses it a thousand times. Append-only output already on disk — two formats, one an interoperable standard.
export.jsonlDesc: One line per question, with shown candidates, relevant ids, expected answer and judge suggestions.
export.qrelsDesc: Standard information-retrieval format — readable by ranx, pytrec_eval, Ragas.

config.overline: The contract · a single coupling point
config.title: Retrieval contract
config.intro: Any backend exposing a conformant <chip>POST /retrieve</chip> becomes usable. Touchstone proxies: the auth header never leaves the server.
config.declareNote: Equivalent to an entry under <mono>backends:</mono> in the YAML below — the file stays the source of truth, the UI edits it. The auth secret lives on the server, never in the browser.
